questions.question: What was the landmark in the image original main function?
questions.expected: The Brandenburg Gate originally served as a ceremonial city gate and entrance to Berlin
questions.rgb_answer: The Brandenburg Gate was originally a triumphal arch, intended to celebrate the victory of King Frederick William I of Prussia over the French in 1760. It was designed by architect Johann Gottfried Herren von Knobelsdorff and sculpted by Johann Gottlieb Friedrich Dürck. The gate was part of the city wall of Berlin and was meant to commemorate the Prussian victory and the military prowess of the king. Over time, it has become a symbol of Berlin and Germany, as well as a popular tourist attraction.
